title: Members Roles
lede: Give each group member a Contact Center role that controls what they can see and do. This guide explains the four roles, how the effective role is decided, and the option to keep the web dialer always open.

steps.title: Open the Members Roles tab
steps.content:
1. Go to Call Center → Contact Center → Groups.
  2. Select a group, then open its Members Roles tab.
  3. Each member appears with a Contact Center role dropdown.

images.caption: Members Roles: pick a Contact Center role per member.
Members Roles: pick a Contact Center role per member.

steps.title: Understand the four roles
steps.content:
• Agent — a regular rep who makes and receives calls and handles customers. Sees only their own queue.
  • Monitor / QA — can listen to calls and review reporting and recordings, but cannot manage agents or change settings.
  • Supervisor — manages agents: listen, whisper, barge, force status, and review performance.
  • Admin — full Contact Center access including the Groups and Call Transfer settings.

Pick a role from each member's dropdown.

steps.title: How the effective role is decided
steps.content:
A person can belong to several groups. Their effective role is the highest role across all their groups. For example, an Agent in one group who is a Supervisor in another is treated as a Supervisor everywhere.

The tenant main account is always Admin and cannot be downgraded here.

steps.title: Keep the web dialer always open
steps.content:
Under Keep the web dialer always open for, check the roles that should have the web dialer auto-opened and pinned. Members with a checked effective role can't close the dialer, so they never miss a queued call.

Leave all boxes unchecked for the normal behavior (the dialer opens only when they click the phone button). This isn't enforced on phones or small tablets. Click Save when done.
